surface_verification.justification: No runtime surface: the change gitignores a transient scratch file (design-feedback-round*.md) in the canonical .shipwright/ ignore block (template + framework .gitignore, kept congruent) and adds a documentation note to the design review-loop reference. No API/UI/CLI/IO code path is exercised. Verified via the gitignore drift + propagation suites (69 related tests passed) and an empirical git check-ignore probe.

behaviors.behavior: design-feedback-round*.md is gitignored as transient scratch while durable design artifacts (screens/*.html, design-manifest.md, visual-guidelines.md) stay tracked
behaviors.evidence: empirical `git check-ignore -v` probe: design-feedback-round1.md AND design-feedback-round12.md matched .gitignore rule (line 181); screens/03-dashboard.html and design-manifest.md did NOT match (still tracked)

behaviors.behavior: The SSoT gitignore template and the framework's own .gitignore managed block stay congruent so the rule propagates to consuming projects
behaviors.evidence: shared/tests/test_gitignore_template_congruent.py (4 passed, incl. test_template_and_framework_block_are_congruent) + full gitignore suite 56 passed

behaviors.behavior: The review-loop.md doc note is well-formed and its cross-reference link resolves
The doc-note prose accurately describes the single-session WebUI review-viewer hosting flow
behaviors.reason: prose correctness is a human review judgment; there is no runtime behavior in this monorepo to assert (the WebUI behavior lives in the shipwright-webui repo)
